items.text: Welcome to PlanRef. This is the full guide to Class Q — the permitted development right that turns farm buildings into homes. <break time="0.7s" /> So — what exactly does Class Q do? <break time="0.4s" /> In essence, it grants planning permission to change the use of certain agricultural buildings into dwellinghouses... together with the building work that's reasonably necessary to convert them. And since the 21st of May, 2024, it also allows limited, single-storey rear extensions. <break time="0.6s" /> Why does it exist? <break time="0.4s" /> The purpose is simple: to release redundant farm buildings for housing — without testing the merits of a home in the countryside against the development plan. <break time="0.7s" /> But here's the thing every practitioner learns — usually the hard way. Class Q is the most litigated, and the most technically demanding, of all the Part 3 rights. It attracts far more than its share of prior approval appeals. And more than any other permitted development right... it rewards forensic preparation. Because eligibility turns on history. On fact. On structure. And those things have to be evidenced — not asserted.

Welcome to PlanRef. This is the full guide to Class Q — the permitted development right that turns farm buildings into homes.

So — what exactly does Class Q do?

In essence, it grants planning permission to change the use of certain agricultural buildings into dwellinghouses... together with the building work that's reasonably necessary to convert them. And since the 21st of May, 2024, it also allows limited, single-storey rear extensions.

Why does it exist?

The purpose is simple: to release redundant farm buildings for housing — without testing the merits of a home in the countryside against the development plan.

But here's the thing every practitioner learns — usually the hard way. Class Q is the most litigated, and the most technically demanding, of all the Part 3 rights. It attracts far more than its share of prior approval appeals. And more than any other permitted development right... it rewards forensic preparation. Because eligibility turns on history. On fact. On structure. And those things have to be evidenced — not asserted.
